sourceMovie.title: Mirage
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Mirage for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Christophe Beaucourt keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Beasts
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mirage clicked for you, The Beasts (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mirage?
faq.answer: Try The Beasts, A Perfect Man, Intruders—each shares DNA with Mirage while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mirage?
faq.answer: Mirage (2018) is best described as Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mirage worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
